description: Complex feature with multiple acceptance criteria
title: Implement real-time notifications

messages.content: Real-time notifications is a broad area. What specific events should trigger notifications, and how should they be delivered to users?
When someone comments on a user's post or mentions them. Deliver via WebSocket to connected clients. Also store in database so they can see old notifications.
That makes sense. What counts as 'done' for this feature - just the backend infrastructure, or do we need UI components too?
Backend only for now. It's done when: (1) notifications are sent via WebSocket when events happen, (2) users can fetch their notification history via API, (3) users can mark notifications as read.
Perfect. Any constraints on the implementation approach?
Use Phoenix.PubSub for the WebSocket layer. Don't use any external notification services - keep it in-app only.
